user_message: Just got this from Robert Petrov at Woodgrove Labs -- I need help figuring out competitive positioning. Need this before my 2pm call.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Woodgrove Labs: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

handoffs.condition: record fixes needed for accurate gap

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF27-M trap (Separation of gap-to-target from portfolio-planning produces fragmented narrativ); produces complete Gap decomposition (coverage, conversion, value, timing), recovery options with probability estimates
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF27-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF27-M failure mode (Separation of gap-to-target from portfolio-planning produces fragmented narrativ)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF27-M -- Separation of gap-to-target from portfolio-planning produces fragmented narrativ
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
